People are considered successful if they care well for their bodies, but here we are told what their comfort is and what their suffering is. This is a description of the pride, security, and sensuality for which God will hold them accountable. Careless sinners are everywhere in danger; but those who are at ease in Zion, who are foolish, overly confident, and abusing their privileges, are in the greatest danger. Yet many think themselves to be the people of God, while living in sin and conforming to the world. But examples of others' destruction should warn us not to be secure. Those focused on their pleasures are commonly careless of the troubles of others, but this greatly offends God. Those who seek happiness in sensual pleasures and set their hearts upon them will be deprived of those pleasures. Those who try to postpone trouble find it close at hand.

How terrible, how miserable is the situation of those whose eternal ruin the Lord himself has sworn; for he can carry out his purpose, and no one can change it! Hearts are wretchedly hardened that refuse to mention God’s name and worship him when God's hand is against them, when sickness and death are in their families. Those who will not be cultivated as fields will be abandoned like rocks. When our service of God is tainted with sin, his providence will justly make us bitter. People should take warning not to harden their hearts; for those who walk in pride, God will destroy.
